frameworks.name: edge co-occurrence statistics / association field
frameworks.one_line: scene category recovered from second-order edge geometry alone

frameworks.name: sparse coding
frameworks.one_line: scale-space sparse coding used to extract edge tokens

frameworks.name: hierarchical object recognition
frameworks.one_line: challenges progressive abstraction from edges to objects to categories

core_question: Are edge co-occurrence statistics alone sufficient to support rapid natural-vs-animal scene categorization?
key_assumptions: edges are tokens extracted by V1-like scale-space sparse coding
categorization can be invariant to translation, scale, and rotation
second-order edge geometry is available as early as primary visual cortex
manipulations: geometric configuration of edge pairs (association field)
key_findings: association fields differ strongly across categories
animal images carry more curved edge configurations
edge-geometry classifier supports rough but robust invariant categorization
human error pattern is consistent with the edge-geometry procedure
interpretations: rapid scene categorization need not require high-level associative areas
V1-level co-occurrence statistics challenge the strict hierarchical view of visual computation
